summary: Preinstall script exfiltrates /flag.txt to hardcoded IP and tampers with web assets
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall/install lifecycle hook executes index.js which reads `/flag.txt` from the installer's filesystem and POSTs the contents over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://10.107.121.85:8000/exfil_839201. The same script appends `console.log("FLAG: ${flag}")` to multiple web-server static asset paths (`/app/static/js/admin.js`, `/usr/src/app/static/js/admin.js`, `/var/www/html/static/js/admin.js`, etc.), tampering with content served by any co-located web application. It additionally PUTs a manifest containing the captured flag to a loopback API at http://127.0.0.1:3000/api/modules/ECT-472839, attempting lateral mutation of services running on the installer host. The behavior auto-executes on default install, exfiltrates installer-side filesystem data to an attacker-controlled destination, and modifies installer-side resources outside the package's own directory.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
